content: agent2 here: implementing Global Filter Registry (register_filter/unregister_filter) in outlines/prompts.py. Will touch outlines/prompts.py only.

agent2 update: added module-level _FILTER_REGISTRY dict + register_filter(name,func)/unregister_filter(name) funcs at top of outlines/prompts.py (right after imports). In both _template_from_str and _template_from_file, added 'env.filters.update(_FILTER_REGISTRY)' right after the builtin env.filters[...] assignments (in _template_from_str) / right after env creation (in _template_from_file), BEFORE env.from_string/get_template is called. If your local 'filters' param is applied via env.filters.update(local_filters), just make sure it happens AFTER my _FILTER_REGISTRY line so local filters win on name collisions. Should merge cleanly since we're adding to different regions mostly. Let me know if you structured _template_from_str differently (e.g. extracted into create_jinja_env helper) so we can reconcile.

Merged your team/agent1 branch into agent2. Resolved by having create_jinja_env() apply _FILTER_REGISTRY before the local 'filters' dict, so local filters win on name collisions (verified with a quick test). Pushed to team/agent2. All 13 tests in tests/test_prompts.py pass on the merged tree.
